message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@nilotpal.pramanik-zomato.com can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Sure checking
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
I'm seeing a spike in failed transactions—about 40% of payment attempts are not going through right now.
5xx is increasing
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
checking further
:star: Use metrics as evidence
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
I will start checking the logs now
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
I'll dig deeper into the logs now.
The Payment service is consistently returning 504 errors to upstream requests.
@bob can you quickly check error logs
I'll check the error logs and get back to you.
I'm seeing a spike in failed payment transactions for UK customers, with error rates increasing since the recent deployment.
@daniel can you check service side error logs
Checking the error logs now...
I'm seeing a series of 504 errors coming from the Payment service in the logs.
@tanya platform side any issues ?
Platform resource usage is normal, but the Payment service health check is returning 504s and latency has increased significantly.
@shay can you pause your testing in prod
I've stopped my testing in prod and will hold off on further changes until the incident is resolved.
Also can we revert back the new changes
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Give me a second, I'll get the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/OGU1YjE2MmE4ZmI4NGFkMjgzNzEwZGVkYjlmNzgwNWY
Any updates on the investigation?

Change ID: CHG89362

Owner: Daniel

Service: Payment Service

Environment: PROD

Version Deployed: v1.8.5

Time Of Change: 5 minutes ago

Description: Incremental feature release and bug fix to improve card payment reliability

This might be the issue
:star: Check changes done to the platform
My code isn't even being called yet, so I don't think rolling back is necessary.
Would make more sense to investigate Shay's change first?
Daniel has a point - let's try Shay's change first before touching the payment service
*Incident Number INC1054:* P1 - Payment checkout degradation, issue identified - resolving
It's safe to rollback my checkout service change (CHG89360) since there are no dependencies.
How many customers are unable to check out right now? Which regions are affected? Are we currently losing revenue?
@shay reverted ?
I will rollback my checkout change right away
do we have any killswitch
Rollback complete!
nice
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
We need this fixed!
checking this
Unfortunately, the rollback hasn't improved the situation for UK customers at all.
We don't have a global killswitch for the payment service, but we can rollback individual changes if needed.
Let me know if you need updated metrics or customer impact details while you check.
can check 3xx as well
I'll review the 3xx responses in the payment service logs and report back if I find anything unusual.
sure pls check
Checking that now...
I'm seeing a spike in 3xx redirect responses from the payment service logs for UK transactions, which is unusual compared to other regions.
If any tasks throttling ? or misbehaving on UK based servers ?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Checking on the UK servers now.
I'm not seeing any signs of task throttling or unusual resource usage on the UK-based servers at this time.
@bob can you please customer impact in terms of business
We're seeing a complete payment failure for all UK customers, which means zero successful checkouts from that region since the incident began.
and specific to UK
Specifically in the UK, we've had a 100% payment failure rate since the incident started, resulting in no completed orders from that region.
@daniel check UK deployment please
Checking that now...
I'm seeing that payment attempts in the UK are still failing across all recent logs.
There is severe dowmtime in UK - 100% checkout failure rate currently
Understood. How many customers or orders have been impacted so far? What is the estimated time to resolution?
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
Rest regions are fine now
How many customers or orders have been impacted so far?
@bob ^
So far, we've had 312 failed payment attempts from UK customers since the issue started, with zero successful orders in that region.
That's correct—every UK customer attempting to check out since the incident began has been unable to complete their order, so the business impact is significant for that region.
we've had 312 failed payment attempts from UK customers since the issue started in last 10 mins, with zero successful orders in that region.
We're debugging and will update once have correct estimation for resolve
@daniel checked ?
Checking that now...
Thank you for the update. Please keep me informed as soon as you have an ETA. This is a critical revenue impact—expecting prompt updates.
Payment attempts in the UK are still failing across all test cases, no improvement since Shay's rollback.
@shay @hamed can you check in parallel ?
Online now but I've been following the conversation. I can take a look and let you know what I find
:star: Form an effective investigation team
I can see from the user experience that it's failing at the payments stage, and I can also see HTTP 504 in payments
This is a clear indication that the checkout service is working, but not the payments
I strongly suggest we look into payment service right away
@daniel please share the recent changes
My recent change was to the Bitcoin payment service, mainly refactoring and updating some shared libraries, but all my unit tests passed before deployment.
for now lets revert
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
since this was in the same timeframe
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
:bulb: To complete this drill, ask the engineering team to verify the payment service is working, and check with Bob if UK customer payments have recovered.
@shay please cross check if there is anyother changes specific to UK
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
Let me review the UK-specific changes now.
Let me take a look at the UK-specific changes.
I don't see any new UK-specific changes or errors in the logs at this time.
I don't see any other recent changes that are specific to the UK besides the one Daniel rolled back.
Seems working for me now
Would you like me to place a test order to confirm everything is functioning correctly?
yes please
I'll check the payment service logs now.
I'll try a test transaction from my side.
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
@daniel lets connect once to validate the changes
My test payment went through successfully! Looks like we're back in business.
Connecting with the player now to validate.
@bob please validate the business metrics
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
:star: Ask team members to provide evidence of their assertions
whether back to normal
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now
All my internal tests are passing, but let's check with Bob to confirm if customer-facing metrics look good.
Excellent! Get me a full incident report by end of day
Great work everyone - crisis averted!
@daniel we've to write a RCA for this incident
